hypothesis: If a diffusion model conditioned on adsorbate identity, adsorption energy, and allowed elements is trained in the CIAE latent space with OC20 pretraining and HEA fine-tuning, then it will generate shell-wise catalytic surface compositions that closely match the real distribution (low Fréchet distance), satisfy chemical constraints (low leakage), and exhibit high point-wise quality (high cosine similarity) relative to a chemically-agnostic baseline, with data-efficient performance across varying training-data fractions.
